comments: Tommy does not like Celtic Frost it seems. Listen after he speaks about Coroner late in the show.

Introduction – “Well hello there, this is TV on the radio, Thomas Vance, the music vendor and welcome to The Friday Rock Show. Right squire, on the programme tonight we have a really good session from a band called First Offence who had an album out some time ago that we heavily featured on this programme, First Offence our session band tonight on the rock show. And there’s more… Got a good single out by the Hellfire Club, also from Candlemass, something from them, House of Lords, another track from their excellent, excellent album, Nightranger, Joe Satriani, Realm, Coroner, Hawkwind, Crimson Glory, we’ve got the lot! Including a lovely little Christmas ditty by King Diamond... But we start with a couple of tracks from the brand new double album from Pink Floyd; 'Comfortably Numb', followed by 'Us and Them'”.

track_listing.track: ‘Us and Them’ (LP-A Delicate Sound of Thunder) (EMI)

track_listing.artist: Competition for a piece of artwork from Poison – Question

track_listing.track: ‘The Biz Suxx’ (LP-The Biz Suxx (But We Don’t Care)) (FM Revolver)
‘Kiss Me Where it Hurts’ (LP-Man in Motion) (Camel/MCA)
Competition ‘Freaks’ (for 7” singles) – "What is the name of the artist who draws the jester?"
Competition for Crimson Glory signed album & t-shirts – “Where was Midnight living when the band found him and took him on as the lead singer?”
‘Let’s Put The X in Sex’ (LP-Smashes, Thrashes & Hits) (Mercury)
End of show credits (including “Alan Freeman is coming back to Radio 1!”)
